# Electronic Artefacts

> Electronic Artefacts is an independent creative technology studio for complex digital products, knowledge systems, proprietary software and research-led cultural work. The public site is a static knowledge graph with canonical HTML pages, stable entity identifiers, JSON-LD, search documents and graph exports.

Use canonical HTML URLs for human-facing citations. Use identifier routes under /id/ and JSON-LD alternates for entity-level retrieval. The public graph is in English and is safe to crawl for search, retrieval and citation when robots.txt allows it.

## Primary Entry Points

- [Home](https://electronicartefacts.com/): Studio overview and main navigation.
- [Knowledge](https://electronicartefacts.com/knowledge/): Canonical knowledge graph hub.
- [Concepts](https://electronicartefacts.com/knowledge/concepts/): Definitions for core concepts.
- [Technologies](https://electronicartefacts.com/knowledge/technologies/): Technical standards, platforms and protocols.
- [Publications](https://electronicartefacts.com/publications/): Research notes and technical articles.
- [Research Questions](https://electronicartefacts.com/research/questions/): Active studio research questions connected to software, concepts and evidence.
- [Projects](https://electronicartefacts.com/projects.html): Selected work and case studies.
- [Programs](https://electronicartefacts.com/programs.html): Software programs and runtime systems.
- [Search](https://electronicartefacts.com/search/?q=knowledge%20graph): Public site search endpoint with q parameter.
- [Contact](https://electronicartefacts.com/contact.html): Contact route for collaboration and inquiry.

## Machine-Readable Resources

- [Sitemap](https://electronicartefacts.com/sitemap.xml): Indexable public URLs.
- [Agent manifest](https://electronicartefacts.com/agent-manifest.json): Structured map of public records and retrieval resources.
- [Full LLM index](https://electronicartefacts.com/llms-full.txt): Complete public record list with summaries and identifier links.
- [OpenSearch](https://electronicartefacts.com/opensearch.xml): Search description for /search/?q={searchTerms}.
- [Knowledge graph catalog JSON-LD](https://electronicartefacts.com/graph/catalog.jsonld): DataCatalog/Dataset description of the public graph.
- [Search documents](https://electronicartefacts.com/search/documents.json): Public retrieval corpus used by site search.
- [Public catalog](https://electronicartefacts.com/generated/public/catalog.json): Public entities, routes and relations.
- [Graph entities](https://electronicartefacts.com/graph/entities.json): Public entity catalog export.
- [Graph relations](https://electronicartefacts.com/graph/relations.json): Public relation export.

## Canonical Records

- [Voice Capture Studio](https://electronicartefacts.com/projects/voice-capture-studio/): A local-first recording studio for speech datasets, voice-over and dubbing workflows, built as an open-source browser application with structured WAV and metadata exports.
- [Electronic Artefacts](https://electronicartefacts.com/organizations/electronic-artefacts/): Strategy, design, engineering and cultural research brought together to build clear, durable digital products and knowledge systems.
- [VASTE](https://electronicartefacts.com/programs/vaste/): The canonical public record of VASTE: its runtime architecture, primitives, governed execution, extension system, Assisted Boot, experimental .vast format, validation record...
- [Vestiges](https://electronicartefacts.com/projects/v6/): The public Vestiges landing page presents the proposition, method and participation paths. This Electronic Artefacts record documents its frame, technical foundations and...
- [AI Agents vs AI Workflows](https://electronicartefacts.com/publications/ai-agents-vs-ai-workflows/): Understand the difference between AI agents and AI workflows, when to use each architecture, and how to design reliable hybrid systems.
- [Retrieval-Augmented Generation and Knowledge Systems](https://electronicartefacts.com/publications/retrieval-augmented-generation-and-knowledge-systems/): Learn how retrieval-augmented generation works and how to build source-grounded RAG systems with metadata, knowledge graphs and evaluation.
- [AI Agent](https://electronicartefacts.com/knowledge/concepts/ai-agent/): An AI agent is a software system in which a model can select actions, use tools, observe results and adapt its next steps toward an objective within defined boundaries.
- [Knowledge Graph](https://electronicartefacts.com/knowledge/concepts/knowledge-graph/): A knowledge graph is a structured network of entities, identifiers and typed relationships that makes knowledge navigable, queryable and reusable across contexts.
- [Retrieval-Augmented Generation](https://electronicartefacts.com/knowledge/concepts/retrieval-augmented-generation/): Retrieval-augmented generation is an architecture that retrieves external information at query time and supplies selected evidence to a generative model before or during...
- [Model Context Protocol](https://electronicartefacts.com/knowledge/technologies/model-context-protocol/): A technology record for MCP as an integration protocol for tool-using AI applications, resources and contextual workflows.
- [Voice Capture Studio](https://electronicartefacts.com/fr/projects/voice-capture-studio/): Un studio d’enregistrement local-first pour datasets vocaux, voix off et doublage, construit comme une application de navigateur open source avec exports WAV et métadonnées...
- [Comment les datasets vocaux peuvent-ils devenir reproductibles, structurés et privacy-first ?](https://electronicartefacts.com/fr/research/questions/speech-dataset-reproducibility/): Question de recherche publique d'Electronic Artefacts sur les datasets vocaux reproductibles, l'enregistrement local-first et les workflows vocaux riches en métadonnées.
- [Comment un logiciel peut-il devenir explicable par son propre graphe de connaissance ?](https://electronicartefacts.com/fr/research/questions/self-explaining-knowledge-graph-software/): Question de recherche publique d'Electronic Artefacts sur les logiciels explicables, les graphes de connaissance, la documentation générée et les surfaces publiques sémantiques.
- [Comment une IA peut-elle comprendre quelqu'un dès sa première conversation ?](https://electronicartefacts.com/fr/research/questions/first-conversation-understanding/): Question de recherche publique d'Electronic Artefacts sur la compréhension au premier contact, l'onboarding conversationnel et le contexte utilisateur structuré en graphe.
- [How can an AI understand someone during its very first conversation?](https://electronicartefacts.com/research/questions/first-conversation-understanding/): A public Electronic Artefacts research question about first-contact understanding, conversational onboarding and graph-shaped user context.
- [How can software become explainable through its own knowledge graph?](https://electronicartefacts.com/research/questions/self-explaining-knowledge-graph-software/): A public Electronic Artefacts research question about self-explaining software, knowledge graphs, generated documentation and semantic public surfaces.
- [How can speech datasets become reproducible, structured and privacy-first?](https://electronicartefacts.com/research/questions/speech-dataset-reproducibility/): A public Electronic Artefacts research question about reproducible speech datasets, local-first recording and metadata-rich voice workflows.
- [Electronic Artefacts](https://electronicartefacts.com/fr/organizations/electronic-artefacts/): Stratégie, design, ingénierie et recherche culturelle réunis pour construire des produits numériques et des systèmes de connaissance clairs et durables.
